data.title: Orbitals team lists Neon Genesis Evangelion and Dragon Ball as inspirations, says no generative AI was used
data.incipit: Ani-making its way to Switch 2 this Summer Grab a partner and prepare for an intergalactic experience like no other – the retro anime-inspired two-player co-operative adventure Orbitals launches exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 this summer. Earlier today brought us a new gameplay trailer to check out, and now the team
data.body: Ani-making its way to Switch 2 this Summer Grab a partner and prepare for an intergalactic experience like no other – the retro anime-inspired two-player co-operative adventure Orbitals launches exclusively on Nintendo Switch 2 this summer. Earlier today brought us a new gameplay trailer to check out, and now the team behind the game is sharing some of their inspiration, and also making a pledge that will no doubt make gamers happy. In a chat with Crunchyroll , Creative director Marcos Ramos spoke about some of the anime inspirations the game is channeling when it comes to not just visuals, but story. Ramos said that Dragon Ball was both an aesthetic and atmospheric inspiration, along with Neon Genesis Evangelion, which the team dev was inspired by to use their game’s setting and characters as vehicles to tell an impactful story. There’s no doubt the most eye-catching part of Orbitals are the retro anime visuals, which are downright breathtaking. Unfortunately, those visuals also brought with them some worries from a few gamers, as they thought that some sort of generative AI might be at play in order to nail that look. Ramos was very clear on this worry, saying that “personally and company-wide, we don’t use any kind of generative AI.” Ramos went on to say that the team has “a passion for making things with our own hands and getting dirty.” Orbitals invites co-op buddies to take on the roles of Maki and Omura as they take to the stars in a last-ditch effort to save their home from a dangerous cosmic storm. Together, they will leap through dangerous caverns, pilot their spaceship through perilous asteroid fields, go head-to-head in unique minigames and use their wits to overcome every obstacle in their path – and have fun along the way. Players can connect in various ways using their Nintendo Switch 2 console. Either by teaming up with two pairs of Joy-ConTM 2 controllers on a single Nintendo Switch 2 device for split-screen local co-op, or using the system’s GameShare feature to share Orbitals with another player on a compatible Nintendo Switch 2 or Nintendo Switch device and taking advantage of the Nintendo Switch 2’s built-in microphone and GameChat, players around the world can team up and save the day together as one inseparable team.
